---
title: "Day 7: Naples — Herculaneum"
subtitle: "Ancient Roman City & Archaeology"
date: "2026-06-11"
day-of-week: "Thursday"
location: "Naples & Herculaneum"
categories: [itinerary, naples, herculaneum, archaeology]
---

## Overview

**First full day in Naples.** Today's highlight is Herculaneum — an ancient Roman city buried in volcanic mud by Vesuvius in 79 AD, better preserved than Pompeii and more emotionally powerful. A licensed guide makes the difference here.

---

## Morning: Herculaneum Tour (Depart ~8–9 AM)

### Tour Details

**See:** [Tours Research - Herculaneum](../research/tours.qmd#herculaneum-archaeological-tour)

**Meeting:**
- Check tour confirmation for exact time and meeting point
- Likely hotel pickup OR meet at Ercolano Scavi train station
- Bring: comfortable shoes, water, sun protection, camera

**Getting There (if independent):**
- Circumvesuviana train from Napoli Porta Nolana or Centrale to **Ercolano Scavi** (~20 min)
- €2.80 one-way; runs every 20–30 minutes
- The station literally overlooks the excavated city

**At Herculaneum (~4–5 hours total):**

- Licensed archaeologist guide (worth every euro — the context transforms the experience)
- 2-hour guided tour of ruins

**What You'll See:**

| Site | Why It Matters |
|------|----------------|
| **The Boat Houses** | 300 skeletons of people who fled to the shore — most powerful moment on the site |
| **House of Neptune & Amphitrite** | Vivid blue-and-gold mosaics, astonishingly preserved |
| **Casa dei Cervi** | Elite villa with original frescoes and garden statues |
| **Thermopolium** | Ancient fast-food counter with food residue still identifiable |
| **Wooden structures** | Actual original wood — doors, furniture, beams — impossible at Pompeii |

**Why Better than Pompeii:**
- Upper floors of buildings are intact (Pompeii's collapsed)
- Organic materials preserved — wood, cloth, bread, wine
- Far less crowded
- More manageable size (2 hours vs. a full day)
- More emotionally powerful: the boat houses are unlike anything else in Italy

---

## Afternoon Options (2–6 PM)

**Option A: MANN Museum** (Recommended if energy remains)

**See:** [Museums - MANN](../research/museums.qmd#mann)

- The National Archaeological Museum holds the treasures *removed* from Herculaneum and Pompeii
- **Go AFTER Herculaneum** — the context transforms the objects completely
- Highlights: Farnese Collection, Alexander Mosaic, Secret Cabinet (erotica from Pompeii)
- Allow 2–3 hours; €18 entry; closed Tuesdays
- Located in the Museo/Cavour area, easy taxi or metro from Ercolano

**Option B: Cappella Sansevero**

**See:** [Museums - Cappella Sansevero](../research/museums.qmd#cappella-sansevero)

- The Veiled Christ — marble veil so lifelike people have refused to believe it's stone
- **Book timed entry in advance** — tiny chapel, very limited slots
- 30–45 minutes; €9 entry; no photography (seriously enforced)
- In the historic center, easy to combine with an evening walk

**Option C: Rest**

Herculaneum is intense — emotionally and physically. Coming back to the hotel, resting, and saving the museums for tomorrow is completely valid.

---

## Evening: Naples (7–10 PM)

**Spaccanapoli District:**
- The ancient straight street cutting through historic Naples
- Via dei Tribunali (the pizza street — different pizzerias than last night)
- Street life, churches, hanging laundry, presepi workshops

**Dinner:**
- Try a different pizzeria if you loved last night's
- Or seafood — Naples is a port city and the catch is fresh
- Sfogliatella for dessert if you haven't yet

---

## Notes & Reflections

**Herculaneum Experience:**

**Most Powerful Moment:**

**Understanding of Ancient Rome:**

---

**Previous:** [Day 6: Florence to Naples](day-06-2026-06-10-florence.qmd) | **Next:** [Day 8: Naples Museums & City](day-08-2026-06-12-naples-herculaneum.qmd)
